chapters.zh: 神既在古时藉著众先知多次多方的晓谕列祖，
chapters.en: God, who at sundry times and in divers manners spake in time past unto the fathers by the prophets,

chapters.zh: 就在这末世藉著他儿子晓谕我们；又早已立他为承受万有的，也曾藉著他创造诸世界。
chapters.en: Hath in these last days spoken unto us by Son, whom he hath appointed heir of all things, by whom also he made the worlds;

chapters.zh: 他是神荣耀所发的光辉，是神本体的真像，常用他权能的命令托住万有。他洗净了人的罪，就坐在高天至大者的右边。
chapters.en: Who being the brightness of glory, and the express image of his person, and upholding all things by the word of his power, when he had by himself purged our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high;

chapters.zh: 他所承受的名，既比天使的名更尊贵，就远超过天使。
chapters.en: Being made so much better than the angels, as he hath by inheritance obtained a more excellent name than they.

chapters.zh: 所有的天使，神从来对那一个说，你是我的儿子，我今日生你？又指著那一个说：我要作他的父，他要作我的子？
chapters.en: For unto which of the angels said he at any time, Thou art my Son, this day have I begotten thee? And again, I will be to him a Father, and he shall be to me a Son?

chapters.zh: 再者，神使长子到世上来的时候（或作：神再使长子到世上来的时候），就说：神的使者都要拜他。
chapters.en: And again, when he bringeth in the firstbegotten into the world, he saith, And let all the angels of God worship him.

chapters.zh: 论到使者，又说：神以风为使者，以火燄为仆役；
chapters.en: And of the angels he saith, Who maketh his angels spirits, and his ministers a flame of fire.

chapters.zh: 论到子却说：神阿，你的宝座是永永远远的；你的国权是正直的。
chapters.en: But unto the Son , Thy throne, O God, for ever and ever: a sceptre of righteousness the sceptre of thy kingdom.

chapters.zh: 你喜爱公义，恨恶罪恶；所以神，就是你的神，用喜乐油膏你，胜过膏你的同伴；
chapters.en: Thou hast loved righteousness, and hated iniquity; therefore God, thy God, hath anointed thee with the oil of gladness above thy fellows.

chapters.zh: 又说：主阿，你起初立了地的根基；天也是你手所造的。
chapters.en: And, Thou, Lord, in the beginning hast laid the foundation of the earth; and the heavens are the works of thine hands:

chapters.zh: 天地都要灭没，你却要长存。天地都要像衣服渐渐旧了；
chapters.en: They shall perish; but thou remainest; and they all shall wax old as doth a garment;

chapters.zh: 你要将天地卷起来，像一件外衣，天地就都改变了。惟有你永不改变；你的年数没有穷尽。
chapters.en: And as a vesture shalt thou fold them up, and they shall be changed: but thou art the same, and thy years shall not fail.

chapters.zh: 所有的天使，神从来对那一个说：你坐在我的右边，等我使你仇敌作你的脚凳？
chapters.en: But to which of the angels said he at any time, Sit on my right hand, until I make thine enemies thy footstool?

chapters.zh: 天使岂不都是服役的灵、奉差遣为那将要承受救恩的人效力么？
chapters.en: Are they not all ministering spirits, sent forth to minister for them who shall be heirs of salvation?

chapters.zh: 所以，我们当越发郑重所听见的道理，恐怕我们随流失去。
chapters.en: Therefore we ought to give the more earnest heed to the things which we have heard, lest at any time we should let slip.

chapters.zh: 那藉著天使所传的话既是确定的；凡犯悖逆的都受了该受的报应。
chapters.en: For if the word spoken by angels was stedfast, and every transgression and disobedience received a just recompence of reward;

chapters.zh: 我们若忽略这么大的救恩，怎能逃罪呢？这救恩起先是主亲自讲的，后来是听见的人给我们证实了。
chapters.en: How shall we escape, if we neglect so great salvation; which at the first began to be spoken by the Lord, and was confirmed unto us by them that heard ;

chapters.zh: 神又按自己的旨意，用神迹、奇事和百般的异能，并圣灵的恩赐，同他们作见证。
chapters.en: God also bearing witness, both with signs and wonders, and with divers miracles, and gifts of the Holy Ghost, according to his own will?

chapters.zh: 我们所说将来的世界，神原没有交给天使管辖。
chapters.en: For unto the angels hath he not put in subjection the world to come, whereof we speak.

chapters.zh: 但有人在经上某处证明说：人算甚么，你竟顾念他？世人算甚么，你竟眷顾他？
chapters.en: But one in a certain place testified, saying, What is man, that thou art mindful of him? or the son of man, that thou visitest him?

chapters.zh: 你叫他比天使微小一点（或作：你叫他暂时比天使小），赐他荣耀尊贵为冠冕，并将你手所造的都派他管理，
chapters.en: Thou madest him a little lower than the angels; thou crownedst him with glory and honour, and didst set him over the works of thy hands:

chapters.zh: 叫万物都服在他的脚下。既叫万物都服他，就没有剩下一样不服他的。只是如今我们还不见万物都服他。
chapters.en: Thou hast put all things in subjection under his feet. For in that he put all in subjection under him, he left nothing not put under him. But now we see not yet all things put under him.

chapters.zh: 惟独见那成为比天使小一点的耶稣（或作：惟独见耶稣暂时比天使小）；因为受死的苦，就得了尊贵荣耀为冠冕，叫他因著神的恩，为人人尝了死味。
chapters.en: But we see Jesus, who was made a little lower than the angels for the suffering of death, crowned with glory and honour; that he by the grace of God should taste death for every man.

chapters.zh: 原来那为万物所属为万物所本的，要领许多的儿子进荣耀里去，使救他们的元帅，因受苦难得以完全，本是合宜的。
chapters.en: For it became him, for whom all things, and by whom all things, in bringing many sons unto glory, to make the captain of their salvation perfect through sufferings.

chapters.zh: 因那使人成圣的和那些得以成圣的，都是出于一。所以，他称他们为弟兄也不以为耻，
chapters.en: For both he that sanctifieth and they who are sanctified all of one: for which cause he is not ashamed to call them brethren,

chapters.zh: 说：我要将你的名传与我的弟兄，在会中我要颂扬你；
chapters.en: Saying, I will declare thy name unto my brethren, in the midst of the church will I sing praise unto thee.

chapters.zh: 又说：我要倚赖他；又说：看哪，我与神所给我的儿女。
chapters.en: And again, I will put my trust in him. And again, Behold I and the children which God hath given me.

chapters.zh: 儿女既同有血肉之体，他也照样亲自成了血肉之体，特要藉著死败坏那掌死权的，就是魔鬼，
chapters.en: Forasmuch then as the children are partakers of flesh and blood, he also himself likewise took part of the same; that through death he might destroy him that had the power of death, that is, the devil;

chapters.zh: 并要释放那些一生因怕死而为奴仆的人。
chapters.en: And deliver them who through fear of death were all their lifetime subject to bondage.

chapters.zh: 他并不救拔天使，乃是救拔亚伯拉罕的后裔。
chapters.en: For verily he took not on angels; but he took on the seed of Abraham.

chapters.zh: 所以，他凡事该与他的弟兄相同，为要在神的事上成为慈悲忠信的大祭司，为百姓的罪献上挽回祭。
chapters.en: Wherefore in all things it behoved him to be made like unto brethren, that he might be a merciful and faithful high priest in things to God, to make reconciliation for the sins of the people.

chapters.zh: 他自己既然被试探而受苦，就能搭救被试探的人。
chapters.en: For in that he himself hath suffered being tempted, he is able to succour them that are tempted.

chapters.zh: 同蒙天召的圣洁弟兄阿，你们应当思想我们所认为使者、为大祭司的耶稣。
chapters.en: Wherefore, holy brethren, partakers of the heavenly calling, consider the Apostle and High Priest of our profession, Christ Jesus;

chapters.zh: 他为那设立他的尽忠，如同摩西在神的全家尽忠一样。
chapters.en: Who was faithful to him that appointed him, as also Moses in all his house.

chapters.zh: 他比摩西算是更配多得荣耀，好像建造房屋的比房屋更尊荣；
chapters.en: For this was counted worthy of more glory than Moses, inasmuch as he who hath builded the house hath more honour than the house.

chapters.zh: 因为房屋都必有人建造，但建造万物的就是神。
chapters.en: For every house is builded by some ; but he that built all things God.

chapters.zh: 摩西为仆人，在神的全家诚然尽忠，为要证明将来必传说的事。
chapters.en: And Moses verily faithful in all his house, as a servant, for a testimony of those things which were to be spoken after;

chapters.zh: 但基督为儿子，治理神的家；我们若将可夸的盼望和胆量坚持到底，便是他的家了。
chapters.en: But Christ as a son over his own house; whose house are we, if we hold fast the confidence and the rejoicing of the hope firm unto the end.

chapters.zh: 圣灵有话说：你们今日若听他的话，
chapters.en: Wherefore (as the Holy Ghost saith, To day if ye will hear his voice,

chapters.zh: 就不可硬著心，像在旷野惹他发怒、试探他的时候一样。
chapters.en: Harden not your hearts, as in the provocation, in the day of temptation in the wilderness:

chapters.zh: 在那里，你们的祖宗试我探我，并且观看我的作为有四十年之久。
chapters.en: When your fathers tempted me, proved me, and saw my works forty years.

chapters.zh: 所以，我厌烦那世代的人，说：他们心里常常迷糊，竟不晓得我的作为！
chapters.en: Wherefore I was grieved with that generation, and said, They do alway err in heart; and they have not known my ways.

chapters.zh: 我就在怒中起誓说；他们断不可进入我的安息。
chapters.en: So I sware in my wrath, They shall not enter into my rest.)

chapters.zh: 弟兄们，你们要谨慎，免得你们中间或有人存著不信的恶心，把永生神离弃了。
chapters.en: Take heed, brethren, lest there be in any of you an evil heart of unbelief, in departing from the living God.

chapters.zh: 总要趁著还有今日，天天彼此相劝，免得你们中间有人被罪迷惑，心里就刚硬了。
chapters.en: But exhort one another daily, while it is called To day; lest any of you be hardened through the deceitfulness of sin.

chapters.zh: 我们若将起初确实的信心坚持到底，就在基督里有分了。
chapters.en: For we are made partakers of Christ, if we hold the beginning of our confidence stedfast unto the end;

chapters.zh: 经上说：你们今日若听他的话，就不可硬著心，像惹他发怒的日子一样。
chapters.en: While it is said, To day if ye will hear his voice, harden not your hearts, as in the provocation.

chapters.zh: 那时，听见他话惹他发怒的是谁呢？岂不是跟著摩西从埃及出来的众人么？
chapters.en: For some, when they had heard, did provoke: howbeit not all that came out of Egypt by Moses.

chapters.zh: 神四十年之久，又厌烦谁呢？岂不是那些犯罪、尸首倒在旷野的人么？
chapters.en: But with whom was he grieved forty years? not with them that had sinned, whose carcases fell in the wilderness?

chapters.zh: 又向谁起誓，不容他们进入他的安息呢；岂不是向那些不信从的人么？
chapters.en: And to whom sware he that they should not enter into his rest, but to them that believed not?

chapters.zh: 这样看来，他们不能进入安息是因为不信的缘故了。
chapters.en: So we see that they could not enter in because of unbelief.

chapters.zh: 我们既蒙留下，有进入他安息的应许，就当畏惧，免得我们（原文是你们）中间或有人似乎是赶不上了。
chapters.en: Let us therefore fear, lest, a promise being left of entering into his rest, any of you should seem to come short of it.

chapters.zh: 因为有福音传给我们，像传给他们一样；只是所听见的道与他们无益，因为他们没有信心与所听见的道调和。
chapters.en: For unto us was the gospel preached, as well as unto them: but the word preached did not profit them, not being mixed with faith in them that heard .

chapters.zh: 但我们已经相信的人得以进入那安息，正如神所说：我在怒中起誓说：他们断不可进入我的安息！其实造物之工，从创世以来已经成全了。
chapters.en: For we which have believed do enter into rest, as he said, As I have sworn in my wrath, if they shall enter into my rest: although the works were finished from the foundation of the world.

chapters.zh: 论到第七日，有一处说，到第七日神就歇了他一切的工。
chapters.en: For he spake in a certain place of the seventh on this wise, And God did rest the seventh day from all his works.

chapters.zh: 又有一处说：他们断不可进入我的安息！
chapters.en: And in this again, If they shall enter into my rest.

chapters.zh: 既有必进安息的人，那先前听见福音的，因为不信从，不得进去。
chapters.en: Seeing therefore it remaineth that some must enter therein, and they to whom it was first preached entered not in because of unbelief:

chapters.zh: 所以过了多年，就在大卫的书上，又限定一日，如以上所引的说：你们今日若听他的话，就不可硬著心。
chapters.en: Again, he limiteth a certain day, saying in David, To day, after so long a time; as it is said, To day if ye will hear his voice, harden not your hearts.

chapters.zh: 若是约书亚已叫他们享了安息，后来神就不再提别的日子了。
chapters.en: For if Jesus had given them rest, then would he not afterward have spoken of another day.

chapters.zh: 这样看来，必另有一安息日的安息为神的子民存留。
chapters.en: There remaineth therefore a rest to the people of God.

chapters.zh: 因为那进入安息的，乃是歇了自己的工，正如神歇了他的工一样。
chapters.en: For he that is entered into his rest, he also hath ceased from his own works, as God from his.

chapters.zh: 所以，我们务必竭力进入那安息，免得有人学那不信从的样子跌倒了。
chapters.en: Let us labour therefore to enter into that rest, lest any man fall after the same example of unbelief.

chapters.zh: 神的道是活泼的，是有功效的，比一切两刃的剑更快，甚至魂与灵，骨节与骨髓，都能刺入、剖开，连心中的思念和主意都能辨明。
chapters.en: For the word of God quick, and powerful, and sharper than any twoedged sword, piercing even to the dividing asunder of soul and spirit, and of the joints and marrow, and a discerner of the thoughts and intents of the heart.

chapters.zh: 并且被造的没有一样在他面前不显然的；原来万物在那与我们有关系的主眼前，都是赤露敞开的。
chapters.en: Neither is there any creature that is not manifest in his sight: but all things naked and opened unto the eyes of him with whom we have to do.

chapters.zh: 我们既然有一位已经升入高天尊荣的大祭司，就是神的儿子耶稣，便当持定所承认的道。
chapters.en: Seeing then that we have a great high priest, that is passed into the heavens, Jesus the Son of God, let us hold fast profession.

chapters.zh: 因我们的大祭司并非不能体恤我们的软弱。他也曾凡事受过试探，与我们一样，只是他没有犯罪。
chapters.en: For we have not an high priest which cannot be touched with the feeling of our infirmities; but was in all points tempted like as without sin.

chapters.zh: 所以，我们只管坦然无惧的来到施恩的宝座前，为要得怜恤，蒙恩惠，作随时的帮助。
chapters.en: Let us therefore come boldly unto the throne of grace, that we may obtain mercy, and find grace to help in time of need.

chapters.zh: 凡从人间挑选的大祭司，是奉派替人办理属神的事，为要献上礼物和赎罪祭（或作：要为罪献上礼物和祭物）。
chapters.en: For every high priest taken from among men is ordained for men in things to God, that he may offer both gifts and sacrifices for sins:

chapters.zh: 他能体谅那愚蒙的和失迷的人，因为他自己也是被软弱所困。
chapters.en: Who can have compassion on the ignorant, and on them that are out of the way; for that he himself also is compassed with infirmity.

chapters.zh: 故此，他理当为百姓和自己献祭赎罪。
chapters.en: And by reason hereof he ought, as for the people, so also for himself, to offer for sins.

chapters.zh: 这大祭司的尊荣，没有人自取。惟要蒙神所召，像亚伦一样。
chapters.en: And no man taketh this honour unto himself, but he that is called of God, as Aaron.

chapters.zh: 如此，基督也不是自取荣耀作大祭司，乃是在乎向他说你是我的儿子，我今日生你的那一位；
chapters.en: So also Christ glorified not himself to be made an high priest; but he that said unto him, Thou art my Son, to day have I begotten thee.

chapters.zh: 就如经上又有一处说：你是照著麦基洗德的等次永远为祭司。
chapters.en: As he saith also in another , Thou a priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec.

chapters.zh: 基督在肉体的时候，既大声哀哭，流泪祷告，恳求那能救他免死的主，就因他的虔诚蒙了应允。
chapters.en: Who in the days of his flesh, when he had offered up prayers and supplications with strong crying and tears unto him that was able to save him from death, and was heard in that he feared;

chapters.zh: 他虽然为儿子，还是因所受的苦难学了顺从。
chapters.en: Though he were a Son, yet learned he obedience by the things which he suffered;

chapters.zh: 他既得以完全，就为凡顺从他的人成了永远得救的根源、
chapters.en: And being made perfect, he became the author of eternal salvation unto all them that obey him;

chapters.zh: 并蒙神照著麦基洗德的等次称他为大祭司。
chapters.en: Called of God an high priest after the order of Melchisedec.

chapters.zh: 论到麦基洗德，我们有好些话，并且难以解明，因为你们听不进去。
chapters.en: Of whom we have many things to say, and hard to be uttered, seeing ye are dull of hearing.

chapters.zh: 看你们学习的工夫，本该作师傅，谁知还得有人将神圣言小学的开端另教导你们，并且成了那必须吃奶，不能吃干粮的人。
chapters.en: For when for the time ye ought to be teachers, ye have need that one teach you again which the first principles of the oracles of God; and are become such as have need of milk, and not of strong meat.

chapters.zh: 凡只能吃奶的都不熟练仁义的道理，因为他是婴孩；
chapters.en: For every one that useth milk unskilful in the word of righteousness: for he is a babe.

chapters.zh: 惟独长大成人的才能吃干粮；他们的心窍习练得通达，就能分辨好歹了。
chapters.en: But strong meat belongeth to them that are of full age, those who by reason of use have their senses exercised to discern both good and evil.

chapters.zh: 所以，我们应当离开基督道理的开端，竭力进到完全的地步，不必再立根基，就如那懊悔死行，信靠神、
chapters.en: Therefore leaving the principles of the doctrine of Christ, let us go on unto perfection; not laying again the foundation of repentance from dead works, and of faith toward God,

chapters.zh: 各样洗礼、按手之礼、死人复活，以及永远审判各等教训。
chapters.en: Of the doctrine of baptisms, and of laying on of hands, and of resurrection of the dead, and of eternal judgment.

chapters.zh: 神若许我们，我们必如此行。
chapters.en: And this will we do, if God permit.

chapters.zh: 论到那些已经蒙了光照、尝过天恩的滋味、又于圣灵有分，
chapters.en: For impossible for those who were once enlightened, and have tasted of the heavenly gift, and were made partakers of the Holy Ghost,

chapters.zh: 并尝过神善道的滋味、觉悟来世权能的人，
chapters.en: And have tasted the good word of God, and the powers of the world to come,

chapters.zh: 若是离弃道理，就不能叫他们从新懊悔了。因为他们把神的儿子重钉十字架，明明的羞辱他。
chapters.en: If they shall fall away, to renew them again unto repentance; seeing they crucify to themselves the Son of God afresh, and put to an open shame.

chapters.zh: 就如一块田地，吃过屡次下的雨水，生长菜蔬，合乎耕种的人用，就从神得福；
chapters.en: For the earth which drinketh in the rain that cometh oft upon it, and bringeth forth herbs meet for them by whom it is dressed, receiveth blessing from God:

chapters.zh: 若长荆棘和蒺藜，必被废弃，近于咒诅，结局就是焚烧。
chapters.en: But that which beareth thorns and briers rejected, and nigh unto cursing; whose end to be burned.

chapters.zh: 亲爱的弟兄们，我们虽是这样说，却深信你们的行为强过这些，而且近乎得救。
chapters.en: But, beloved, we are persuaded better things of you, and things that accompany salvation, though we thus speak.

chapters.zh: 因为神并非不公义，竟忘记你们所做的工和你们为他名所显的爱心，就是先前伺候圣徒，如今还是伺候。
chapters.en: For God not unrighteous to forget your work and labour of love, which ye have shewed toward his name, in that ye have ministered to the saints, and do minister.

chapters.zh: 我们愿你们各人都显出这样的殷勤，使你们有满足的指望，一直到底。
chapters.en: And we desire that every one of you do shew the same diligence to the full assurance of hope unto the end:

chapters.zh: 并且不懈怠，总要效法那些凭信心和忍耐承受应许的人。
chapters.en: That ye be not slothful, but followers of them who through faith and patience inherit the promises.

chapters.zh: 当初神应许亚伯拉罕的时候，因为没有比自己更大可以指著起誓的，就指著自己起誓，说：
chapters.en: For when God made promise to Abraham, because he could swear by no greater, he sware by himself,

chapters.zh: 论福，我必赐大福给你；论子孙，我必叫你的子孙多起来。
chapters.en: Saying, Surely blessing I will bless thee, and multiplying I will multiply thee.

chapters.zh: 这样，亚伯拉罕既恒久忍耐，就得了所应许的。
chapters.en: And so, after he had patiently endured, he obtained the promise.

chapters.zh: 人都是指著比自己大的起誓，并且以起誓为实据，了结各样的争论。
chapters.en: For men verily swear by the greater: and an oath for confirmation to them an end of all strife.

chapters.zh: 照样，神愿意为那承受应许的人格外显明他的旨意是不更改的，就起誓为证。
chapters.en: Wherein God, willing more abundantly to shew unto the heirs of promise the immutability of his counsel, confirmed by an oath:

chapters.zh: 藉这两件不更改的事，神决不能说谎，好叫我们这逃往避难所、持定摆在我们前头指望的人可以大得勉励。
chapters.en: That by two immutable things, in which impossible for God to lie, we might have a strong consolation, who have fled for refuge to lay hold upon the hope set before us:

chapters.zh: 我们有这指望，如同灵魂的锚，又坚固又牢靠，且通入幔内。
chapters.en: Which we have as an anchor of the soul, both sure and stedfast, and which entereth into that within the veil;

chapters.zh: 作先锋的耶稣，既照著麦基洗德的等次成了永远的大祭司，就为我们进入幔内。
chapters.en: Whither the forerunner is for us entered, Jesus, made an high priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec.

chapters.zh: 这麦基洗德就是撒冷王，又是至高神的祭司，本是长远为祭司的。他当亚伯拉罕杀败诸王回来的时候，就迎接他，给他祝福。
chapters.en: For this Melchisedec, king of Salem, priest of the most high God, who met Abraham returning from the slaughter of the kings, and blessed him;

chapters.zh: 亚伯拉罕也将自己所得来的，取十分之一给他。他头一个名繙出来就是仁义王，他又名撒冷王，就是平安王的意思。
chapters.en: To whom also Abraham gave a tenth part of all; first being by interpretation King of righteousness, and after that also King of Salem, which is, King of peace;

chapters.zh: 他无父，无母，无族谱，无生之始，无命之终，乃是与神的儿子相似。
chapters.en: Without father, without mother, without descent, having neither beginning of days, nor end of life; but made like unto the Son of God; abideth a priest continually.

chapters.zh: 你们想一想，先祖亚伯拉罕将自己所掳来上等之物取十分之一给他，这人是何等尊贵呢！
chapters.en: Now consider how great this man , unto whom even the patriarch Abraham gave the tenth of the spoils.

chapters.zh: 那得祭司职任的利未子孙，领命照例向百姓取十分之一，这百姓是自己的弟兄，虽是从亚伯拉罕身（原文是腰）中生的，还是照例取十分之一；
chapters.en: And verily they that are of the sons of Levi, who receive the office of the priesthood, have a commandment to take tithes of the people according to the law, that is, of their brethren, though they come out of the loins of Abraham:

chapters.zh: 独有麦基洗德，不与他们同谱，倒收纳亚伯拉罕的十分之一，为那蒙应许的亚伯拉罕祝福。
chapters.en: But he whose descent is not counted from them received tithes of Abraham, and blessed him that had the promises.

chapters.zh: 从来位分大的给位分小的祝福，这是驳不倒的理。
chapters.en: And without all contradiction the less is blessed of the better.

chapters.zh: 在这里收十分之一的都是必死的人；但在那里收十分之一的，有为他作见证的说，他是活的；
chapters.en: And here men that die receive tithes; but there he , of whom it is witnessed that he liveth.

chapters.zh: 并且可说那受十分之一的利未，也是藉著亚伯拉罕纳了十分之一。
chapters.en: And as I may so say, Levi also, who receiveth tithes, payed tithes in Abraham.

chapters.zh: 因为麦基洗德迎接亚伯拉罕的时候，利未已经在他先祖的身（原文是腰）中。
chapters.en: For he was yet in the loins of his father, when Melchisedec met him.

chapters.zh: 从前百姓在利未人祭司职任以下受律法，倘若藉这职任能得完全，又何用另外兴起一位祭司，照麦基洗德的等次，不照亚伦的等次呢？
chapters.en: If therefore perfection were by the Levitical priesthood, (for under it the people received the law,) what further need that another priest should rise after the order of Melchisedec, and not be called after the order of Aaron?

chapters.zh: 祭司的职任既已更改，律法也必须更改。
chapters.en: For the priesthood being changed, there is made of necessity a change also of the law.

chapters.zh: 因为这话所指的人本属别的支派，那支派里从来没有一人伺候祭坛。
chapters.en: For he of whom these things are spoken pertaineth to another tribe, of which no man gave attendance at the altar.

chapters.zh: 我们的主分明是从犹大出来的；但这支派，摩西并没有提到祭司。
chapters.en: For evident that our Lord sprang out of Juda; of which tribe Moses spake nothing concerning priesthood.

chapters.zh: 倘若照麦基洗德的样式，另外兴起一位祭司来，我的话更是显而易见的了。
chapters.en: And it is yet far more evident: for that after the similitude of Melchisedec there ariseth another priest,

chapters.zh: 他成为祭司，并不是照属肉体的条例，乃是照无穷（原文是不能毁坏）之生命的大能。
chapters.en: Who is made, not after the law of a carnal commandment, but after the power of an endless life.

chapters.zh: 因为有给他作见证的说：你是照著麦基洗德的等次永远为祭司。
chapters.en: For he testifieth, Thou a priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec.

chapters.zh: 先前的条例，因软弱无益，所以废掉了，
chapters.en: For there is verily a disannulling of the commandment going before for the weakness and unprofitableness thereof.

chapters.zh: （律法原来一无所成）就引进了更美的指望；靠这指望，我们便可以进到神面前。
chapters.en: For the law made nothing perfect, but the bringing in of a better hope ; by the which we draw nigh unto God.

chapters.zh: 再者，耶稣为祭司，并不是不起誓立的。
chapters.en: And inasmuch as not without an oath :

chapters.zh: 至于那些祭司，原不是起誓立的，只有耶稣是起誓立的；因为那立他的对他说：主起了誓，决不后悔，你是永远为祭司。
chapters.en: (For those priests were made without an oath; but this with an oath by him that said unto him, The Lord sware and will not repent, Thou a priest for ever after the order of Melchisedec:)

chapters.zh: 既是起誓立的，耶稣就作了更美之约的中保。
chapters.en: By so much was Jesus made a surety of a better testament.

chapters.zh: 那些成为祭司的，数目本来多，是因为有死阻隔，不能长久。
chapters.en: And they truly were many priests, because they were not suffered to continue by reason of death:

chapters.zh: 这位既是永远常存的，他祭司的职任就长久不更换。
chapters.en: But this , because he continueth ever, hath an unchangeable priesthood.

chapters.zh: 凡靠著他进到神面前的人，他都能拯救到底；因为他是长远活著，替他们祈求。
chapters.en: Wherefore he is able also to save them to the uttermost that come unto God by him, seeing he ever liveth to make intercession for them.

chapters.zh: 像这样圣洁、无邪恶、无玷污、远离罪人、高过诸天的大祭司，原是与我们合宜的。
chapters.en: For such an high priest became us, holy, harmless, undefiled, separate from sinners, and made higher than the heavens;

chapters.zh: 他不像那些大祭司，每日必须先为自己的罪，后为百姓的罪献祭；因为他只一次将自己献上，就把这事成全了。
chapters.en: Who needeth not daily, as those high priests, to offer up sacrifice, first for his own sins, and then for the people's: for this he did once, when he offered up himself.

chapters.zh: 律法本是立软弱的人为大祭司；但在律法以后起誓的话，是立儿子为大祭司，乃是成全到永远的。
chapters.en: For the law maketh men high priests which have infirmity; but the word of the oath, which was since the law, the Son, who is consecrated for evermore.

chapters.zh: 我们所讲的事，其中第一要紧的，就是我们有这样的大祭司，已经坐在天上至大者宝座的右边，
chapters.en: Now of the things which we have spoken the sum: We have such an high priest, who is set on the right hand of the throne of the Majesty in the heavens;

chapters.zh: 在圣所，就是真帐幕里，作执事；这帐幕是主所支的，不是人所支的。
chapters.en: A minister of the sanctuary, and of the true tabernacle, which the Lord pitched, and not man.

chapters.zh: 凡大祭司都是为献礼物和祭物设立的，所以这位大祭司也必须有所献的。
chapters.en: For every high priest is ordained to offer gifts and sacrifices: wherefore of necessity that this man have somewhat also to offer.

chapters.zh: 他若在地上，必不得为祭司，因为已经有照律法献礼物的祭司。
chapters.en: For if he were on earth, he should not be a priest, seeing that there are priests that offer gifts according to the law:

chapters.zh: 他们供奉的事本是天上事的形状和影像，正如摩西将要造帐幕的时候，蒙神警戒他，说：你要谨慎，作各样的物件都要照著在山上指示你的样式。
chapters.en: Who serve unto the example and shadow of heavenly things, as Moses was admonished of God when he was about to make the tabernacle: for, See, saith he, thou make all things according to the pattern shewed to thee in the mount.

chapters.zh: 如今耶稣所得的职任是更美的，正如他作更美之约的中保；这约原是凭更美之应许立的。
chapters.en: But now hath he obtained a more excellent ministry, by how much also he is the mediator of a better covenant, which was established upon better promises.

chapters.zh: 那前约若没有瑕疵，就无处寻求后约了。
chapters.en: For if that first had been faultless, then should no place have been sought for the second.

chapters.zh: 所以主指责他的百姓说（或作：所以主指前约的缺欠说）：日子将到，我要与以色列家和犹大家另立新约，
chapters.en: For finding fault with them, he saith, Behold, the days come, saith the Lord, when I will make a new covenant with the house of Israel and with the house of Judah:

chapters.zh: 不像我拉著他们祖宗的手，领他们出埃及的时候，与他们所立的约。因为他们不恒心守我的约，我也不理他们。这是主说的。
chapters.en: Not according to the covenant that I made with their fathers in the day when I took them by the hand to lead them out of the land of Egypt; because they continued not in my covenant, and I regarded them not, saith the Lord.

chapters.zh: 主又说：那些日子以后，我与以色列家所立的约乃是这样：我要将我的律法放在他们里面，写在他们心上；我要作他们的神；他们要作我的子民。
chapters.en: For this the covenant that I will make with the house of Israel after those days, saith the Lord; I will put my laws into their mind, and write them in their hearts: and I will be to them a God, and they shall be to me a people:

chapters.zh: 他们不用各人教导自己的乡邻和自己的弟兄，说：你该认识主；因为他们从最小的到至大的，都必认识我。
chapters.en: And they shall not teach every man his neighbour, and every man his brother, saying, Know the Lord: for all shall know me, from the least to the greatest.

chapters.zh: 我要宽恕他们的不义，不再记念他们的罪愆。
chapters.en: For I will be merciful to their unrighteousness, and their sins and their iniquities will I remember no more.

chapters.zh: 既说新约。就以前约为旧了；但那渐旧渐衰的，就必快归无有了。
chapters.en: In that he saith, A new , he hath made the first old. Now that which decayeth and waxeth old ready to vanish away.

chapters.zh: 原来前约有礼拜的条例和属世界的圣幕。
chapters.en: Then verily the first had also ordinances of divine service, and a worldly sanctuary.

chapters.zh: 因为有预备的帐幕，头一层叫作圣所，里面有灯台、桌子，和陈设饼。
chapters.en: For there was a tabernacle made; the first, wherein the candlestick, and the table, and the shewbread; which is called the sanctuary.

chapters.zh: 第二幔子后又有一层帐幕，叫作至圣所，
chapters.en: And after the second veil, the tabernacle which is called the Holiest of all;

chapters.zh: 有金香炉（或作：坛），有包金的约柜，柜里有盛吗哪的金罐和亚伦发过芽的杖，并两块约版；
chapters.en: Which had the golden censer, and the ark of the covenant overlaid round about with gold, wherein the golden pot that had manna, and Aaron's rod that budded, and the tables of the covenant;

chapters.zh: 柜上面有荣耀基路伯的影罩著施恩（原文作：蔽罪）座。这几件我现在不能一一细说。
chapters.en: And over it the cherubims of glory shadowing the mercyseat; of which we cannot now speak particularly.

chapters.zh: 这些物件既如此预备齐了，众祭司就常进头一层帐幕，行拜神的礼。
chapters.en: Now when these things were thus ordained, the priests went always into the first tabernacle, accomplishing the service .

chapters.zh: 至于第二层帐幕，惟有大祭司一年一次独自进去，没有不带著血为自己和百姓的过错献上。
chapters.en: But into the second the high priest alone once every year, not without blood, which he offered for himself, and the errors of the people:

chapters.zh: 圣灵用此指明，头一层帐幕仍存的时候，进入至圣所的路还未显明。
chapters.en: The Holy Ghost this signifying, that the way into the holiest of all was not yet made manifest, while as the first tabernacle was yet standing:

chapters.zh: 那头一层帐幕作现今的一个表样，所献的礼物和祭物，就著良心说，都不能叫礼拜的人得以完全。
chapters.en: Which a figure for the time then present, in which were offered both gifts and sacrifices, that could not make him that did the service perfect, as pertaining to the conscience;

chapters.zh: 这些事，连那饮食和诸般洗濯的规矩，都不过是属肉体的条例，命定到振兴的时候为止。
chapters.en: only in meats and drinks, and divers washings, and carnal ordinances, imposed until the time of reformation.

chapters.zh: 但现在基督已经来到，作了将来美事的大祭司，经过那更大更全备的帐幕，不是人手所造、也不是属乎这世界的；
chapters.en: But Christ being come an high priest of good things to come, by a greater and more perfect tabernacle, not made with hands, that is to say, not of this building;

chapters.zh: 并且不用山羊和牛犊的血，乃用自己的血，只一次进入圣所，成了永远赎罪的事。
chapters.en: Neither by the blood of goats and calves, but by his own blood he entered in once into the holy place, having obtained eternal redemption .

chapters.zh: 若山羊和公牛的血，并母牛犊的灰，洒在不洁的人身上，尚且叫人成圣，身体洁净，
chapters.en: For if the blood of bulls and of goats, and the ashes of an heifer sprinkling the unclean, sanctifieth to the purifying of the flesh:

chapters.zh: 何况基督藉著永远的灵，将自己无瑕无疵献给神，他的血岂不更能洗净你们的心（原文是良心），除去你们的死行，使你们事奉那永生神么？
chapters.en: How much more shall the blood of Christ, who through the eternal Spirit offered himself without spot to God, purge your conscience from dead works to serve the living God?

chapters.zh: 为此，他作了新约的中保，既然受死赎了人在前约之时所犯的罪过，便叫蒙召之人得著所应许永远的产业。
chapters.en: And for this cause he is the mediator of the new testament, that by means of death, for the redemption of the transgressions under the first testament, they which are called might receive the promise of eternal inheritance.

chapters.zh: 凡有遗命必须等到留遗命（遗命：原文与约字同）的人死了；
chapters.en: For where a testament , there must also of necessity be the death of the testator.

chapters.zh: 因为人死了，遗命才有效力，若留遗命的尚在，那遗命还有用处么？
chapters.en: For a testament of force after men are dead: otherwise it is of no strength at all while the testator liveth.

chapters.zh: 所以，前约也不是不用血立的；
chapters.en: Whereupon neither the first was dedicated without blood.

chapters.zh: 因为摩西当日照著律法将各样诫命传给众百姓，就拿朱红色绒和牛膝草，把牛犊山羊的血和水洒在书上，又洒在众百姓身上，说：
chapters.en: For when Moses had spoken every precept to all the people according to the law, he took the blood of calves and of goats, with water, and scarlet wool, and hyssop, and sprinkled both the book, and all the people,

chapters.zh: 这血就是神与你们立约的凭据。
chapters.en: Saying, This the blood of the testament which God hath enjoined unto you.

chapters.zh: 他又照样把血洒在帐幕和各样器皿上。
chapters.en: Moreover he sprinkled with blood both the tabernacle, and all the vessels of the ministry.

chapters.zh: 按著律法，凡物差不多都是用血洁净的；若不流血，罪就不得赦免了。
chapters.en: And almost all things are by the law purged with blood; and without shedding of blood is no remission.

chapters.zh: 照著天上样式作的物件必须用这些祭物去洁净；但那天上的本物自然当用更美的祭物去洁净。
chapters.en: therefore necessary that the patterns of things in the heavens should be purified with these; but the heavenly things themselves with better sacrifices than these.

chapters.zh: 因为基督并不是进了人手所造的圣所（这不过是真圣所的影像），乃是进了天堂，如今为我们显在神面前；
chapters.en: For Christ is not entered into the holy places made with hands, the figures of the true; but into heaven itself, now to appear in the presence of God for us:

chapters.zh: 也不是多次将自己献上，像那大祭司每年带著牛羊的血（牛羊的血：原文作不是自己的血）进入圣所，
chapters.en: Nor yet that he should offer himself often, as the high priest entereth into the holy place every year with blood of others;

chapters.zh: 如果这样，他从创世以来，就必多次受苦了。但如今在这末世显现一次，把自己献为祭，好除掉罪。
chapters.en: For then must he often have suffered since the foundation of the world: but now once in the end of the world hath he appeared to put away sin by the sacrifice of himself.

chapters.zh: 按著定命，人人都有一死，死后且有审判。
chapters.en: And as it is appointed unto men once to die, but after this the judgment:

chapters.zh: 像这样，基督既然一次被献，担当了多人的罪，将来要向那等候他的人第二次显现，并与罪无关，乃是为拯救他们。
chapters.en: So Christ was once offered to bear the sins of many; and unto them that look for him shall he appear the second time without sin unto salvation.

chapters.zh: 律法既是将来美事的影儿，不是本物的真像，总不能藉著每年常献一样的祭物叫那近前来的人得以完全。
chapters.en: For the law having a shadow of good things to come, not the very image of the things, can never with those sacrifices which they offered year by year continually make the comers thereunto perfect.

chapters.zh: 若不然，献祭的事岂不早已止住了么？因为礼拜的人，良心既被洁净，就不再觉得有罪了。
chapters.en: For then would they not have ceased to be offered? because that the worshippers once purged should have had no more conscience of sins.

chapters.zh: 但这些祭物是叫人每年想起罪来；
chapters.en: But in those a remembrance again of sins every year.

chapters.zh: 因为公牛和山羊的血，断不能除罪。
chapters.en: For not possible that the blood of bulls and of goats should take away sins.

chapters.zh: 所以基督到世上来的时候，就说：神阿，祭物和礼物是你不愿意的；你曾给我预备了身体。
chapters.en: Wherefore when he cometh into the world, he saith, Sacrifice and offering thou wouldest not, but a body hast thou prepared me:

chapters.zh: 燔祭和赎罪祭是你不喜欢的。
chapters.en: In burnt offerings and for sin thou hast had no pleasure.

chapters.zh: 那时我说：神阿，我来了，为要照你的旨意行；我的事在经卷上已经记载了。
chapters.en: Then said I, Lo, I come (in the volume of the book it is written of me,) to do thy will, O God.

chapters.zh: 以上说：祭物和礼物，燔祭和赎罪祭，是你不愿意的，也是你不喜欢的（这都是按著律法献的）；
chapters.en: Above when he said, Sacrifice and offering and burnt offerings and for sin thou wouldest not, neither hadst pleasure ; which are offered by the law;

chapters.zh: 后又说：我来了为要照你的旨意行；可见他是除去在先的，为要立定在后的。
chapters.en: Then said he, Lo, I come to do thy will, O God. He taketh away the first, that he may establish the second.

chapters.zh: 我们凭这旨意，靠耶稣基督，只一次献上他的身体，就得以成圣。
chapters.en: By the which will we are sanctified through the offering of the body of Jesus Christ once .

chapters.zh: 凡祭司天天站著事奉神，屡次献上一样的祭物，这祭物永不能除罪。
chapters.en: And every priest standeth daily ministering and offering oftentimes the same sacrifices, which can never take away sins:

chapters.zh: 但基督献了一次永远的赎罪祭，就在神的右边坐下了。
chapters.en: But this man, after he had offered one sacrifice for sins for ever, sat down on the right hand of God;

chapters.zh: 从此，等候他仇敌成了他的脚凳。
chapters.en: From henceforth expecting till his enemies be made his footstool.

chapters.zh: 因为他一次献祭，便叫那得以成圣的人永远完全。
chapters.en: For by one offering he hath perfected for ever them that are sanctified.

chapters.zh: 圣灵也对我们作见证；因为他既已说过：
chapters.en: the Holy Ghost also is a witness to us: for after that he had said before,

chapters.zh: 主说：那些日子以后，我与他们所立的约乃是这样：我要将我的律法写在他们心上，又要放在他们的里面。
chapters.en: This the covenant that I will make with them after those days, saith the Lord, I will put my laws into their hearts, and in their minds will I write them;

chapters.zh: 以后就说：我不再记念他们的罪愆和他们的过犯。
chapters.en: And their sins and iniquities will I remember no more.

chapters.zh: 这些罪过既已赦免，就不用再为罪献祭了。
chapters.en: Now where remission of these no more offering for sin.

chapters.zh: 弟兄们，我们既因耶稣的血得以坦然进入至圣所，
chapters.en: Having therefore, brethren, boldness to enter into the holiest by the blood of Jesus,

chapters.zh: 是藉著他给我们开了一条又新又活的路，从幔子经过，这幔子就是他的身体。
chapters.en: By a new and living way, which he hath consecrated for us, through the veil, that is to say, his flesh;

chapters.zh: 又有一位大祭司治理神的家！
chapters.en: And an high priest over the house of God;

chapters.zh: 并我们心中天良的亏欠已经洒去，身体用清水洗净了，就当存著诚心和充足的信心来到神面前；
chapters.en: Let us draw near with a true heart in full assurance of faith, having our hearts sprinkled from an evil conscience, and our bodies washed with pure water.

chapters.zh: 也要坚守我们所承认的指望，不至摇动，因为那应许我们的是信实的。
chapters.en: Let us hold fast the profession of faith without wavering; (for he faithful that promised;)

chapters.zh: 又要彼此相顾，激发爱心，勉励行善。
chapters.en: And let us consider one another to provoke unto love and to good works:

chapters.zh: 你们不可停止聚会，好像那些停止惯了的人，倒要彼此劝勉，既知道（原文是看见）那日子临近，就更当如此。
chapters.en: Not forsaking the assembling of ourselves together, as the manner of some ; but exhorting : and so much the more, as ye see the day approaching.

chapters.zh: 因为我们得知真道以后，若故意犯罪，赎罪的祭就再没有了；
chapters.en: For if we sin wilfully after that we have received the knowledge of the truth, there remaineth no more sacrifice for sins,

chapters.zh: 惟有战惧等候审判和那烧灭众敌人的烈火。
chapters.en: But a certain fearful looking for of judgment and fiery indignation, which shall devour the adversaries.

chapters.zh: 人干犯摩西的律法，凭两三个见证人，尚且不得怜恤而死，
chapters.en: He that despised Moses' law died without mercy under two or three witnesses:

chapters.zh: 何况人践踏神的儿子，将那使他成圣之约的血当作平常，又亵慢施恩的圣灵，你们想，他要受的刑罚该怎样加重呢！
chapters.en: Of how much sorer punishment, suppose ye, shall he be thought worthy, who hath trodden under foot the Son of God, and hath counted the blood of the covenant, wherewith he was sanctified, an unholy thing, and hath done despite unto the Spirit of grace?

chapters.zh: 因为我们知道谁说：伸冤在我，我必报应；又说：主要审判他的百姓。
chapters.en: For we know him that hath said, Vengeance unto me, I will recompense, saith the Lord. And again, The Lord shall judge his people.

chapters.zh: 落在永生神的手里，真是可怕的！
chapters.en: a fearful thing to fall into the hands of the living God.

chapters.zh: 你们要追念往日，蒙了光照以后所忍受大争战的各样苦难：
chapters.en: But call to remembrance the former days, in which, after ye were illuminated, ye endured a great fight of afflictions;

chapters.zh: 一面被毁谤，遭患难，成了戏景，叫众人观看；一面陪伴那些受这样苦难的人。
chapters.en: Partly, whilst ye were made a gazingstock both by reproaches and afflictions; and partly, whilst ye became companions of them that were so used.

chapters.zh: 因为你们体恤了那些被捆锁的人，并且你们的家业被人抢去，也甘心忍受，知道自己有更美长存的家业。
chapters.en: For ye had compassion of me in my bonds, and took joyfully the spoiling of your goods, knowing in yourselves that ye have in heaven a better and an enduring substance. yourselves}

chapters.zh: 所以，你们不可丢弃勇敢的心；存这样的心必得大赏赐。
chapters.en: Cast not away therefore your confidence, which hath great recompence of reward.

chapters.zh: 你们必须忍耐，使你们行完了神的旨意，就可以得著所应许的。
chapters.en: For ye have need of patience, that, after ye have done the will of God, ye might receive the promise.

chapters.zh: 因为还有一点点时候，那要来的就来，并不迟延；
chapters.en: For yet a little while, and he that shall come will come, and will not tarry.

chapters.zh: 只是义人（有古卷：我的义人）必因信得生。他若退后，我心里就不喜欢他。
chapters.en: Now the just shall live by faith: but if draw back, my soul shall have no pleasure in him.

chapters.zh: 我们却不是退后入沉沦的那等人，乃是有信心以致灵魂得救的人。
chapters.en: But we are not of them who draw back unto perdition; but of them that believe to the saving of the soul.

chapters.zh: 信就是所望之事的实底，是未见之事的确据。
chapters.en: Now faith is the substance of things hoped for, the evidence of things not seen.

chapters.zh: 古人在这信上得了美好的证据。
chapters.en: For by it the elders obtained a good report.

chapters.zh: 我们因著信，就知道诸世界是藉神话造成的；这样，所看见的，并不是从显然之物造出来的。
chapters.en: Through faith we understand that the worlds were framed by the word of God, so that things which are seen were not made of things which do appear.

chapters.zh: 亚伯因著信，献祭与神，比该隐所献的更美，因此便得了称义的见证，就是神指他礼物作的见证。他虽然死了，却因这信，仍旧说话。
chapters.en: By faith Abel offered unto God a more excellent sacrifice than Cain, by which he obtained witness that he was righteous, God testifying of his gifts: and by it he being dead yet speaketh.

chapters.zh: 以诺因著信，被接去，不至于见死，人也找不著他，因为神已经把他接去了；只是他被接去以先，已经得了神喜悦他的明证。
chapters.en: By faith Enoch was translated that he should not see death; and was not found, because God had translated him: for before his translation he had this testimony, that he pleased God.

chapters.zh: 人非有信，就不能得神的喜悦；因为到神面前来的人必须信有神，且信他赏赐那寻求他的人。
chapters.en: But without faith impossible to please : for he that cometh to God must believe that he is, and he is a rewarder of them that diligently seek him.

chapters.zh: 挪亚因著信，既蒙神指示他未见的事，动了敬畏的心，预备了一只方舟，使他全家得救。因此就定了那世代的罪，自己也承受了那从信而来的义。
chapters.en: By faith Noah, being warned of God of things not seen as yet, moved with fear, prepared an ark to the saving of his house; by the which he condemned the world, and became heir of the righteousness which is by faith.

chapters.zh: 亚伯拉罕因著信，蒙召的时候就遵命出去，往将来要得为业的地方去；出去的时候，还不知往那里去。
chapters.en: By faith Abraham, when he was called to go out into a place which he should after receive for an inheritance, obeyed; and he went out, not knowing whither he went.

chapters.zh: 他因著信，就在所应许之地作客，好像在异地居住帐棚，与那同蒙一个应许的以撒、雅各一样。
chapters.en: By faith he sojourned in the land of promise, as a strange country, dwelling in tabernacles with Isaac and Jacob, the heirs with him of the same promise:

chapters.zh: 因为他等候那座有根基的城，就是神所经营所建造的。
chapters.en: For he looked for a city which hath foundations, whose builder and maker God.

chapters.zh: 因著信，连撒拉自己，虽然过了生育的岁数，还能怀孕，因他以为那应许他的是可信的。
chapters.en: Through faith also Sara herself received strength to conceive seed, and was delivered of a child when she was past age, because she judged him faithful who had promised.

chapters.zh: 所以从一个彷佛已死的人就生出子孙，如同天上的星那样众多，海边的沙那样无数。
chapters.en: Therefore sprang there even of one, and him as good as dead, as the stars of the sky in multitude, and as the sand which is by the sea shore innumerable.

chapters.zh: 这些人都是存著信心死的，并没有得著所应许的；却从远处望见，且欢喜迎接，又承认自己在世上是客旅，是寄居的。
chapters.en: These all died in faith, not having received the promises, but having seen them afar off, and were persuaded of , and embraced , and confessed that they were strangers and pilgrims on the earth.

chapters.zh: 说这样话的人是表明自己要找一个家乡。
chapters.en: For they that say such things declare plainly that they seek a country.

chapters.zh: 他们若想念所离开的家乡，还有可以回去的机会。
chapters.en: And truly, if they had been mindful of that from whence they came out, they might have had opportunity to have returned.

chapters.zh: 他们却羡慕一个更美的家乡，就是在天上的。所以神被称为他们的神，并不以为耻，因为他已经给他们预备了一座城。
chapters.en: But now they desire a better , that is, an heavenly: wherefore God is not ashamed to be called their God: for he hath prepared for them a city.

chapters.zh: 亚伯拉罕因著信，被试验的时候，就把以撒献上；这便是那欢喜领受应许的，将自己独生的儿子献上。
chapters.en: By faith Abraham, when he was tried, offered up Isaac: and he that had received the promises offered up his only begotten ,

chapters.zh: 论到这儿子，曾有话说：从以撒生的才要称为你的后裔。
chapters.en: Of whom it was said, That in Isaac shall thy seed be called:

chapters.zh: 他以为神还能叫人从死里复活；他也彷佛从死中得回他的儿子来。
chapters.en: Accounting that God able to raise up, even from the dead; from whence also he received him in a figure.

chapters.zh: 以撒因著信，就指著将来的事给雅各、以扫祝福。
chapters.en: By faith Isaac blessed Jacob and Esau concerning things to come.

chapters.zh: 雅各因著信，临死的时候，给约瑟的两个儿子各自祝福，扶著杖头敬拜神。
chapters.en: By faith Jacob, when he was a dying, blessed both the sons of Joseph; and worshipped, upon the top of his staff.

chapters.zh: 约瑟因著信，临终的时候，提到以色列族将来要出埃及，并为自己的骸骨留下遗命。
chapters.en: By faith Joseph, when he died, made mention of the departing of the children of Israel; and gave commandment concerning his bones.

chapters.zh: 摩西生下来，他的父母见他是个俊美的孩子，就因著信，把他藏了三个月，并不怕王命。
chapters.en: By faith Moses, when he was born, was hid three months of his parents, because they saw a proper child; and they were not afraid of the king's commandment.

chapters.zh: 摩西因著信，长大了就不肯称为法老女儿之子。
chapters.en: By faith Moses, when he was come to years, refused to be called the son of Pharaoh's daughter;

chapters.zh: 他宁可和神的百姓同受苦害，也不愿暂时享受罪中之乐。
chapters.en: Choosing rather to suffer affliction with the people of God, than to enjoy the pleasures of sin for a season;

chapters.zh: 他看为基督受的凌辱比埃及的财物更宝贵，因他想望所要得的赏赐。
chapters.en: Esteeming the reproach of Christ greater riches than the treasures in Egypt: for he had respect unto the recompence of the reward.

chapters.zh: 他因著信，就离开埃及，不怕王怒；因为他恒心忍耐，如同看见那不能看见的主。
chapters.en: By faith he forsook Egypt, not fearing the wrath of the king: for he endured, as seeing him who is invisible.

chapters.zh: 他因著信，就守（或作：立）逾越节，行洒血的礼，免得那灭长子的临近以色列人。
chapters.en: Through faith he kept the passover, and the sprinkling of blood, lest he that destroyed the firstborn should touch them.

chapters.zh: 他们因著信，过红海如行干地；埃及人试著要过去，就被吞灭了。
chapters.en: By faith they passed through the Red sea as by dry : which the Egyptians assaying to do were drowned.

chapters.zh: 以色列人因著信，围绕耶利哥城七日，城墙就倒塌了。
chapters.en: By faith the walls of Jericho fell down, after they were compassed about seven days.

chapters.zh: 妓女喇合因著信，曾和和平平的接待探子，就不与那些不顺从的人一同灭亡。
chapters.en: By faith the harlot Rahab perished not with them that believed not, when she had received the spies with peace.

chapters.zh: 我又何必再说呢？若要一一细说，基甸、巴拉、参孙、耶弗他、大卫、撒母耳，和众先知的事，时候就不够了。
chapters.en: And what shall I more say? for the time would fail me to tell of Gedeon, and Barak, and Samson, and Jephthae; David also, and Samuel, and the prophets:

chapters.zh: 他们因著信，制伏了敌国，行了公义，得了应许，堵了狮子的口，
chapters.en: Who through faith subdued kingdoms, wrought righteousness, obtained promises, stopped the mouths of lions,

chapters.zh: 灭了烈火的猛势，脱了刀剑的锋刃；软弱变为刚强，争战显出勇敢，打退外邦的全军。
chapters.en: Quenched the violence of fire, escaped the edge of the sword, out of weakness were made strong, waxed valiant in fight, turned to flight the armies of the aliens.

chapters.zh: 有妇人得自己的死人复活。又有人忍受严刑，不肯苟且得释放（原文是赎），为要得著更美的复活。
chapters.en: Women received their dead raised to life again: and others were tortured, not accepting deliverance; that they might obtain a better resurrection:

chapters.zh: 又有人忍受戏弄、鞭打、捆锁、监禁、各等的磨炼，
chapters.en: And others had trial of mockings and scourgings, yea, moreover of bonds and imprisonment:

chapters.zh: 被石头打死，被锯锯死，受试探，被刀杀，披著绵羊山羊的皮各处奔跑，受穷乏、患难、苦害，
chapters.en: They were stoned, they were sawn asunder, were tempted, were slain with the sword: they wandered about in sheepskins and goatskins; being destitute, afflicted, tormented;

chapters.zh: 在旷野、山岭、山洞、地穴，飘流无定，本是世界不配有的人。
chapters.en: (Of whom the world was not worthy:) they wandered in deserts, and mountains, and dens and caves of the earth.

chapters.zh: 这些人都是因信得了美好的证据，却仍未得著所应许的；
chapters.en: And these all, having obtained a good report through faith, received not the promise:

chapters.zh: 因为神给我们预备了更美的事，叫他们若不与我们同得，就不能完全。
chapters.en: God having provided some better thing for us, that they without us should not be made perfect.

chapters.zh: 我们既有这许多的见证人，如同云彩围著我们，就当放下各样的重担，脱去容易缠累我们的罪，存心忍耐，奔那摆在我们前头的路程，
chapters.en: Wherefore seeing we also are compassed about with so great a cloud of witnesses, let us lay aside every weight, and the sin which doth so easily beset , and let us run with patience the race that is set before us,

chapters.zh: 仰望为我们信心创始成终的耶稣（或作：仰望那将真道创始成终的耶稣）。他因那摆在前面的喜乐，就轻看羞辱，忍受了十字架的苦难，便坐在神宝座的右边。
chapters.en: Looking unto Jesus the author and finisher of faith; who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising the shame, and is set down at the right hand of the throne of God.

chapters.zh: 那忍受罪人这样顶撞的，你们要思想，免得疲倦灰心。
chapters.en: For consider him that endured such contradiction of sinners against himself, lest ye be wearied and faint in your minds.

chapters.zh: 你们与罪恶相争，还没有抵挡到流血的地步。
chapters.en: Ye have not yet resisted unto blood, striving against sin.

chapters.zh: 你们又忘了那劝你们如同劝儿子的话，说：我儿，你不可轻看主的管教，被他责备的时候也不可灰心；
chapters.en: And ye have forgotten the exhortation which speaketh unto you as unto children, My son, despise not thou the chastening of the Lord, nor faint when thou art rebuked of him:

chapters.zh: 因为主所爱的，他必管教，又鞭打凡所收纳的儿子。
chapters.en: For whom the Lord loveth he chasteneth, and scourgeth every son whom he receiveth.

chapters.zh: 你们所忍受的，是神管教你们，待你们如同待儿子。焉有儿子不被父亲管教的呢？
chapters.en: If ye endure chastening, God dealeth with you as with sons; for what son is he whom the father chasteneth not?

chapters.zh: 管教原是众子所共受的，你们若不受管教，就是私子，不是儿子了。
chapters.en: But if ye be without chastisement, whereof all are partakers, then are ye bastards, and not sons.

chapters.zh: 再者，我们曾有生身的父管教我们，我们尚且敬重他，何况万灵的父，我们岂不更当顺服他得生么？
chapters.en: Furthermore we have had fathers of our flesh which corrected , and we gave reverence: shall we not much rather be in subjection unto the Father of spirits, and live?

chapters.zh: 生身的父都是暂随己意管教我们；惟有万灵的父管教我们，是要我们得益处，使我们在他的圣洁上有分。
chapters.en: For they verily for a few days chastened after their own pleasure; but he for profit, that might be partakers of his holiness.

chapters.zh: 凡管教的事，当时不觉得快乐，反觉得愁苦；后来却为那经练过的人结出平安的果子，就是义。
chapters.en: Now no chastening for the present seemeth to be joyous, but grievous: nevertheless afterward it yieldeth the peaceable fruit of righteousness unto them which are exercised thereby.

chapters.zh: 所以，你们要把下垂的手、发酸的腿、挺起来；
chapters.en: Wherefore lift up the hands which hang down, and the feeble knees;

chapters.zh: 也要为自己的脚，把道路修直了，使瘸子不至歪脚（或作：差路），反得痊愈。
chapters.en: And make straight paths for your feet, lest that which is lame be turned out of the way; but let it rather be healed.

chapters.zh: 你们要追求与众人和睦，并要追求圣洁；非圣洁没有人能见主。
chapters.en: Follow peace with all , and holiness, without which no man shall see the Lord:

chapters.zh: 又要谨慎，恐怕有人失了神的恩；恐怕有毒根生出来扰乱你们，因此叫众人沾染污秽；
chapters.en: Looking diligently lest any man fail of the grace of God; lest any root of bitterness springing up trouble , and thereby many be defiled;

chapters.zh: 恐怕有淫乱的，有贪恋世俗如以扫的，他因一点食物把自己长子的名分卖了。
chapters.en: Lest there any fornicator, or profane person, as Esau, who for one morsel of meat sold his birthright.

chapters.zh: 后来想要承受父所祝的福，竟被弃绝，虽然号哭切求，却得不著门路使他父亲的心意回转。这是你们知道的。
chapters.en: For ye know how that afterward, when he would have inherited the blessing, he was rejected: for he found no place of repentance, though he sought it carefully with tears.

chapters.zh: 你们原不是来到那能摸的山；此山有火燄、密云、黑暗、暴风、
chapters.en: For ye are not come unto the mount that might be touched, and that burned with fire, nor unto blackness, and darkness, and tempest,

chapters.zh: 角声与说话的声音。那些听见这声音的，都求不要再向他们说话；
chapters.en: And the sound of a trumpet, and the voice of words; which they that heard intreated that the word should not be spoken to them any more:

chapters.zh: 因为他们当不起所命他们的话，说：靠近这山的，即便是走兽，也要用石头打死。
chapters.en: (For they could not endure that which was commanded, And if so much as a beast touch the mountain, it shall be stoned, or thrust through with a dart:

chapters.zh: 所见的极其可怕，甚至摩西说：我甚是恐惧战兢。
chapters.en: And so terrible was the sight, Moses said, I exceedingly fear and quake:)

chapters.zh: 你们乃是来到锡安山，永生神的城邑，就是天上的耶路撒冷。那里有千万的天使，
chapters.en: But ye are come unto mount Sion, and unto the city of the living God, the heavenly Jerusalem, and to an innumerable company of angels,

chapters.zh: 有名录在天上诸长子之会所共聚的总会，有审判众人的神和被成全之义人的灵魂，
chapters.en: To the general assembly and church of the firstborn, which are written in heaven, and to God the Judge of all, and to the spirits of just men made perfect,

chapters.zh: 并新约的中保耶稣，以及所洒的血；这血所说的比亚伯的血所说的更美。
chapters.en: And to Jesus the mediator of the new covenant, and to the blood of sprinkling, that speaketh better things than Abel.

chapters.zh: 你们总要谨慎，不可弃绝那向你们说话的。因为，那些弃绝在地上警戒他们的尚且不能逃罪，何况我们违背那从天上警戒我们的呢？
chapters.en: See that ye refuse not him that speaketh. For if they escaped not who refused him that spake on earth, much more we , if we turn away from him that from heaven:

chapters.zh: 当时他的声音震动了地，但如今他应许说：再一次我不单要震动地，还要震动天。
chapters.en: Whose voice then shook the earth: but now he hath promised, saying, Yet once more I shake not the earth only, but also heaven.

chapters.zh: 这再一次的话，是指明被震动的，就是受造之物都要挪去，使那不被震动的常存。
chapters.en: And this , Yet once more, signifieth the removing of those things that are shaken, as of things that are made, that those things which cannot be shaken may remain.

chapters.zh: 所以我们既得了不能震动的国，就当感恩，照神所喜悦的，用虔诚、敬畏的心事奉神。
chapters.en: Wherefore we receiving a kingdom which cannot be moved, let us have grace, whereby we may serve God acceptably with reverence and godly fear:

chapters.zh: 因为我们的神乃是烈火。
chapters.en: For our God a consuming fire.

chapters.zh: 你们务要常存弟兄相爱的心。
chapters.en: Let brotherly love continue.

chapters.zh: 不可忘记用爱心接待客旅；因为曾有接待客旅的，不知不觉就接待了天使。
chapters.en: Be not forgetful to entertain strangers: for thereby some have entertained angels unawares.

chapters.zh: 你们要记念被捆绑的人，好像与他们同受捆绑；，也要记念遭苦害的人，想到自己也在肉身之内。
chapters.en: Remember them that are in bonds, as bound with them; them which suffer adversity, as being yourselves also in the body.

chapters.zh: 婚姻，人人都当尊重，床也不可污秽；因为苟合行淫的人，神必要审判。
chapters.en: Marriage honourable in all, and the bed undefiled: but whoremongers and adulterers God will judge.

chapters.zh: 你们存心不可贪爱钱财，要以自己所有的为足；因为主曾说：我总不撇下你，也不丢弃你。
chapters.en: conversation without covetousness; content with such things as ye have: for he hath said, I will never leave thee, nor forsake thee.

chapters.zh: 所以我们可以放胆说：主是帮助我的，我必不惧怕；人能把我怎么样呢？
chapters.en: So that we may boldly say, The Lord my helper, and I will not fear what man shall do unto me.

chapters.zh: 从前引导你们、传神之道给你们的人，你们要想念他们，效法他们的信心，留心看他们为人的结局。
chapters.en: Remember them which have the rule over you, who have spoken unto you the word of God: whose faith follow, considering the end of conversation.

chapters.zh: 耶稣基督，昨日、今日、一直到永远、是一样的。
chapters.en: Jesus Christ the same yesterday, and to day, and for ever.

chapters.zh: 你们不要被那诸般怪异的教训勾引了去；因为人心靠恩得坚固才是好的，并不是靠饮食。那在饮食上专心的从来没有得著益处。
chapters.en: Be not carried about with divers and strange doctrines. For a good thing that the heart be established with grace; not with meats, which have not profited them that have been occupied therein.

chapters.zh: 我们有一祭坛，上面的祭物是那些在帐幕中供职的人不可同吃的。
chapters.en: We have an altar, whereof they have no right to eat which serve the tabernacle.

chapters.zh: 原来牲畜的血被大祭司带入圣所作赎罪祭；牲畜的身子被烧在营外。
chapters.en: For the bodies of those beasts, whose blood is brought into the sanctuary by the high priest for sin, are burned without the camp.

chapters.zh: 所以，耶稣要用自己的血叫百姓成圣，也就在城门外受苦。
chapters.en: Wherefore Jesus also, that he might sanctify the people with his own blood, suffered without the gate.

chapters.zh: 这样，我们也当出到营外，就了他去，忍受他所受的凌辱。
chapters.en: Let us go forth therefore unto him without the camp, bearing his reproach.

chapters.zh: 我们在这里本没有常存的城，乃是寻求那将来的城。
chapters.en: For here have we no continuing city, but we seek one to come.

chapters.zh: 我们应当靠著耶稣，常常以颂赞为祭献给神，这就是那承认主名之人嘴唇的果子。
chapters.en: By him therefore let us offer the sacrifice of praise to God continually, that is, the fruit of lips giving thanks to his name.

chapters.zh: 只是不可忘记行善和捐输的事；因为这样的祭，是神所喜悦的。
chapters.en: But to do good and to communicate forget not: for with such sacrifices God is well pleased.

chapters.zh: 你们要依从那些引导你们的，且要顺服；因他们为你们的灵魂时刻儆醒，好像那将来交账的人。你们要使他们交的时候有快乐，不至忧愁；若忧愁就与你们无益了。
chapters.en: Obey them that have the rule over you, and submit yourselves: for they watch for your souls, as they that must give account, that they may do it with joy, and not with grief: for that unprofitable for you.

chapters.zh: 请你们为我们祷告，因我们自觉良心无亏，愿意凡事按正道而行。
chapters.en: Pray for us: for we trust we have a good conscience, in all things willing to live honestly.

chapters.zh: 我更求你们为我祷告，使我快些回到你们那里去。
chapters.en: But I beseech the rather to do this, that I may be restored to you the sooner.

chapters.zh: 但愿赐平安的神，就是那凭永约之血、使群羊的大牧人 ─ 我主耶稣从死里复活的神，
chapters.en: Now the God of peace, that brought again from the dead our Lord Jesus, that great shepherd of the sheep, through the blood of the everlasting covenant,

chapters.zh: 在各样善事上成全你们，叫你们遵行他的旨意；又藉著耶稣基督在你们心里行他所喜悦的事。愿荣耀归给他，直到永永远远。阿们！
chapters.en: Make you perfect in every good work to do his will, working in you that which is wellpleasing in his sight, through Jesus Christ; to whom glory for ever and ever. Amen.

chapters.zh: 弟兄们，我略略写信给你们，望你们听我劝勉的话。
chapters.en: And I beseech you, brethren, suffer the word of exhortation: for I have written a letter unto you in few words.

chapters.zh: 你们该知道，我们的兄弟提摩太已经释放了；他若快来，我必同他去见你们。
chapters.en: Know ye that brother Timothy is set at liberty; with whom, if he come shortly, I will see you.

chapters.zh: 请你们问引导你们的诸位和众圣徒安。从义大利来的人也问你们安。
chapters.en: Salute all them that have the rule over you, and all the saints. They of Italy salute you.

chapters.zh: 愿恩惠常与你们众人同在。阿们！
chapters.en: Grace with you all. Amen. « »
